steps.title: Find the known total
steps.content:
The 9 listed integers have mean $42$, so their total is:
$$9\cdot42=378$$

steps.title: Use the possible mean of all 10 integers
steps.content:
Let $x$ be the missing integer and let $M$ be the mean of all 10 integers. Since $M$ is an integer greater than $42$:
$$\frac{378+x}{10}=M$$
Each integer is positive and less than $60$, so $x\lt 60$. If $M\ge44$, then $x=10M-378\ge62$, which is too large. Therefore $M=43$.

steps.title: Find the missing value
steps.content:
$$\frac{378+x}{10}=43$$
$$378+x=430$$
$$x=52$$
The largest of the original 9 values is $46$, so the largest integer in data set A is 52.
